status_contract: Counts every vendored lane-local test-* JS/CJS/MJS fixture, then compares that denominator to the documented manifested green subset plus explicit lane classification catalogs. Classified non-green entries are not pass claims; the remaining remainder is intentionally reported as unmanifested_or_unclassified, not as pass or fail.

This official abort fixture asserts host process abort and exit-code behavior. The embedded Node22 runtime lane does not currently own destructive process-abort execution, so it remains a process-host gap rather than a green claim.

This official addon fixture depends on native addon loading in a non-Node context. The current embedded runtime lane does not own native-addon host execution, so it is classified as a loader/native-addon gap rather than an unknown.

Pinned later-family dependency: official test-process-finalization.mjs now runs through the Neovex sync subprocess harness, and the only remaining failure is different-registry-per-thread.mjs because worker_threads are still owned by a later compatibility family

Pinned Node20 divergence: official v20.20.2 PerformanceResourceTiming#toJSON() omits the Node22-era `deliveryType` and `responseStatus` fields that Neovex intentionally keeps in its single Node22-shaped runtime contract

Pinned Node20 divergence: official v20.20.2 test-stream-duplex-readable-end.js still probes the older default-highWaterMark flow-control path, while the current runtime matches the later Node22/Node24 explicit-highWaterMark shape

Pinned Node20 divergence: official v20.20.2 test-stream-transform-split-highwatermark.js still expects the older 16 KiB split Transform default highWaterMark, while the current runtime matches the later Node22/Node24 getDefaultHighWaterMark() contract

Pinned Node20 divergence: official v20.20.2 test-stream-transform-split-objectmode.js still expects the older 16 KiB split Transform default highWaterMark, while the current runtime matches the later Node22/Node24 non-Windows 64 KiB contract

Pinned Node20 divergence: official v20.20.2 test-stream-readable-infinite-read.js still depends on the older default Readable highWaterMark accumulation path, while the current runtime matches the later Node22/Node24 explicit-highWaterMark behavior

Pinned application-profile path-policy divergence: test-fs-open.js expects ENOENT for an absolute missing host path outside the generated bundle root, while Neovex intentionally denies that path before raw host open

Pinned application-profile divergence: test-fs-readdir-buffer.js probes /dev outside the generated bundle root, so the runtime intentionally denies that host path instead of claiming broad host-fs parity

Pinned application-profile divergence: official test-fs-filehandle-use-after-close.js reopens process.execPath outside the generated bundle root, so the runtime intentionally denies that absolute host path before the later EBADF assertion can run

Pinned Node20 validation divergence: test-crypto-authenticated.js still expects the older deprecation-warning ordering without DEP0182
